proposed_hypotheses.text: Overall objective_response rate in the cohort differs from 50% (i.e., the outcome is imbalanced).

At least one of the binary features feature_001, feature_010, feature_005, feature_026, feature_016, feature_025, feature_019, feature_015, feature_014, feature_009, feature_018, feature_008, feature_006, feature_032, feature_021, feature_030 has a non-zero univariate association with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Marginal response rate is 0.1875 (9375/50000); strongly imbalanced (binomial vs 0.5, p<<1e-300).

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df[c], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: Three binary features show large univariate associations: feature_001 (resp 22.1% vs 16.5%, +5.6 pp), feature_014 (23.5% vs 16.7%, +6.8 pp), feature_021 (22.0% vs 16.6%, +5.4 pp). feature_026 (16.8% vs 19.1%, -2.3 pp) and feature_009 (16.6% vs 19.0%, -2.4 pp) are negatively associated. Other binaries show no detectable main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_004 (ordinal, levels 0/1/2) is associated with lower objective_response (monotone negative gradient).
Continuous feature_022 (a likely disease-burden marker) is lower in responders than non-responders.
Continuous feature_024 (a likely disease-burden marker, range 20-100) is lower in responders than non-responders.
Continuous feature_020 (range 0-23.6) is lower in responders.
Continuous feature_027 is lower in responders.
Continuous feature_029 (range 1.7-5.5) is higher in responders.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate falls monotonically with feature_004: 0->23.1% (n=17592), 1->17.4% (n=24971), 2->12.9% (n=7437). Cochran-Armitage trend p<1e-100.
Mean feature_022 is 19.27 in responders vs 20.39 in non-responders (Welch t=-3.79, p=1.5e-4).
Mean feature_024 58.50 (responders) vs 60.30 (non-responders); Welch t=-8.23, p=2.0e-16.
Mean feature_020 3.44 (responders) vs 3.95 (non-responders); Welch t=-12.09, p=1.7e-33. Strongest continuous main effect.
Mean feature_027 5.69 (responders) vs 6.17 (non-responders); Welch t=-4.80, p=1.6e-6.
Mean feature_029 3.824 (responders) vs 3.795 (non-responders); Welch t=5.14, p=2.7e-7. Small magnitude but precise.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous patient feature_011 (likely age, range 18-92) has no detectable main effect on objective_response.

Most other continuous markers (feature_012, feature_028, feature_007, feature_031, feature_013, feature_033, feature_003, feature_035, feature_023, feature_002, feature_017, feature_034) have no univariate main effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: feature_011 means are 67.98 (responders) vs 68.00 (non-responders); t=-0.15, p=0.88. No main effect.

analyses.code: loop ttest by response across remaining continuous features
analyses.result_summary: feature_012 (p=0.92), feature_028 (p=0.15), feature_007 (p=0.05 borderline +1.1), feature_031 (p=0.13), feature_013 (p=0.20), feature_033 (p=0.21), feature_003 (p=0.71), feature_035 (p=0.55), feature_023 (p=0.62), feature_002 (p=0.76), feature_017 (p=0.81), feature_034 (p=0.35). None show meaningful main effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic model that includes all 35 features, feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 each independently increase log-odds of objective_response, while feature_004, feature_026, and feature_009 each independently decrease them.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, X_std).fit() with all 35 features (continuous standardized)
analyses.result_summary: Significant adjusted log-odds (z, p): feature_001 +0.374 (z=15.96), feature_014 +0.436 (z=17.88), feature_021 +0.362 (z=15.44); feature_004 -0.362 (z=-20.43), feature_026 -0.169 (z=-4.99), feature_009 -0.188 (z=-4.64); continuous: feature_020 -0.142 (z=-11.6), feature_024 -0.100 (z=-8.6), feature_029 +0.061 (z=5.3), feature_022 -0.048 (z=-3.8), feature_027 -0.060 (z=-4.5). All others non-significant after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among binary features, feature_016 and feature_025 are mutually exclusive (no patient has both =1), suggesting they encode alternative levels of the same characteristic.
feature_019 and feature_015 are mutually exclusive.
feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 are mutually independent (their joint distribution matches the product of marginals).

analyses.result_summary: Cell (1,1) = 0 vs expected ~563 under independence: feature_016 and feature_025 are perfectly mutually exclusive.
Cell (1,1) = 0 vs expected ~339 under independence: feature_019 and feature_015 perfectly mutually exclusive.

Triple-cell count for (1,1,1) is 2401 vs 50000*0.401*0.300*0.400 = 2406 expected. All 8 cells match expected within sampling noise; chi-square test of three-way independence p>>0.05. The three flags are statistically independent at baseline.

proposed_hypotheses.text: If feature_001 acts as a treatment, its response-modifying effect varies across at least one other feature (heterogeneity).
Specifically, the effect of feature_001 is larger when feature_014=1 than when feature_014=0 (positive interaction).
Specifically, the effect of feature_001 is larger when feature_021=1 than when feature_021=0 (positive interaction).
The effect of feature_001 is reduced when feature_026=1 (negative interaction).
The effect of feature_001 is reduced when feature_009=1 (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: feature_001 x feature_014 interaction coef = +1.060, p=6.8e-103 (huge, positive).
feature_001 x feature_021 interaction coef = +0.883, p=1.1e-78.
feature_001 x feature_026 interaction coef = -0.375, p=3.3e-8 (negative).
feature_001 x feature_009 interaction coef = -0.281, p=5.0e-4 (negative).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 shows the same pattern as feature_001: its effect is amplified by feature_001 and feature_021, attenuated by feature_026 and feature_009.

analyses.code: Logit interaction screen, treating feature_014 as 'treatment' against all other features
analyses.result_summary: Top interactions of feature_014: with feature_001 (+1.060, p=6.8e-103), feature_021 (+1.055, p=5.5e-102), feature_026 (-0.604, p=1.1e-16), feature_009 (-0.468, p=6.8e-8). Symmetric to feature_001 screen. All other terms p>0.005.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_021 shows the same pattern: amplified by feature_001 and feature_014, attenuated by feature_026 and feature_009.

analyses.result_summary: Top interactions of feature_021: with feature_014 (+1.055, p=5.5e-102), feature_001 (+0.883, p=1.1e-78), feature_009 (-0.409, p=4.7e-7), feature_026 (-0.306, p=6.0e-6). Pattern is symmetric across the three flags.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 simultaneously have a much higher response rate than predicted by additive combination of the three main effects (synergistic three-way interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Response rate by joint (feature_001,feature_014,feature_021): (0,0,0)=17.2%, (0,0,1)=16.2%, (0,1,0)=16.0%, (0,1,1)=15.7%, (1,0,0)=16.4%, (1,0,1)=16.9%, (1,1,0)=16.0%, (1,1,1)=63.3% (n=2401). Effect is strictly conjunctive AND, present only when all three flags are =1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive set (feature_001=1 & feature_014=1 & feature_021=1), feature_026=1 attenuates the response benefit (negative modifier within the responsive subgroup).
Within the triple-positive set, feature_009=1 attenuates the response benefit.
Outside the triple-positive set, feature_026 and feature_009 have no detectable effect on response.

analyses.result_summary: Within triple+ (n=2401): feature_026=0 -> 71.7% (n=2025), feature_026=1 -> 18.4% (n=376). Chi-square p=7.6e-86; absolute reduction 53 pp.
Within triple+: feature_009=0 -> 69.1% (n=2125), feature_009=1 -> 18.8% (n=276). Chi-square p=3.3e-59; absolute reduction 50 pp.
Outside triple+ (n=47599): response rate is 16.5% regardless of feature_026 (16.7% vs 16.5%) or feature_009 (16.4% vs 16.5%). Suppressors only matter inside triple+.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The two suppressors act through an OR (either is sufficient to abolish the triple+ benefit), not AND.

analyses.result_summary: Within triple+: (feature_026=0,feature_009=0) -> 78.6% (n=1790); (0,1) -> 18.7% (n=235); (1,0) -> 18.2% (n=335); (1,1) -> 19.5% (n=41). Either suppressor alone collapses response to baseline; double presence is no worse than single. Consistent with logical OR-suppressor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup definition feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 yields a response rate ~80% versus ~17% in the complement.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup n=1790 (3.6% of cohort), response 78.6%; complement n=48210, response 16.5%. Chi-square 4361, p~0; risk difference +62.1 pp; unadjusted OR 18.55.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the strongest baseline prognostic continuous covariates (feature_004, feature_020, feature_024, feature_022, feature_027, feature_029), the subgroup indicator retains an OR > 15 (~independent of measured baseline severity).

analyses.code: Logit(y ~ subgroup + feature_004 + feature_020 + feature_024 + feature_022 + feature_027 + feature_029)
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds for subgroup: 3.002 (SE 0.060, z=50.2, p<<1e-300); adjusted OR=20.13 (95% CI ~17.9-22.6). Adjustments shift covariates only slightly; pseudo R^2 = 0.082.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Removing any one of the five subgroup conditions (f001=1, f014=1, f021=1, f026=0, f009=0) substantially shrinks the response signal, confirming the rule is conjunctive.

analyses.code: Counterfactual subgroup: drop one clause and recompute
analyses.result_summary: Reference subgroup: 78.6% (n=1790). Drop f001: f014=1 & f021=1 & no-suppressors -> 78.6% if intersected differently; the 'with f001=0' arm of biomarker-positive: 15.8% (n=2789). Similar 16-17% when any one clause is removed in the 'broken' direction. Single/double flag combinations among (f001,f014,f021) all show baseline response (16-17%). Each clause is necessary.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the best subgroup, no other binary feature (feature_010, feature_005, feature_016, feature_025, feature_019, feature_015, feature_018, feature_008, feature_006, feature_032, feature_030) further modifies response.

analyses.code: for b in remaining binaries: chi2 within sub
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup (n=1790): all p > 0.10 across remaining binaries (feature_010 p=0.93, feature_005 p=0.76, feature_016 p=0.15, feature_025 p=0.68, feature_019 p=0.13, feature_015 p=0.12, feature_018 p=0.43, feature_008 p=0.71, feature_006 p~1, feature_032 p=0.12, feature_030 p=0.46). No additional binary modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the best subgroup, response rate decreases monotonically with feature_004 (the same direction as the population-level main effect, but the subgroup remains favored at every level).
feature_004 modifies the magnitude of the subgroup effect (effect smaller at higher feature_004).

analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup: feature_004=0 -> 86.0% (n=617); =1 -> 77.2% (n=902); =2 -> 66.8% (n=271). All levels remain far above the 11-21% baseline at the same feature_004 level.
Interaction term subgroup x feature_004 = -0.172, p=0.050. Direction: at higher feature_004, the subgroup OR is smaller (mild attenuation). Effect remains very large at every level.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the best subgroup, higher feature_020 is associated with somewhat lower response (consistent with population-level main effect direction).
Continuous features feature_022, feature_024, feature_027, feature_029, feature_011 do not measurably modify the within-subgroup response.

analyses.code: qcut feature_020 into quartiles within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup: feature_020 quartile 1 (lowest) -> 80.9% (n=903), Q2 -> 79.5% (n=440), Q3 -> 72.9% (n=447). Modest monotone decrease (-8 pp); ttest within sub between responders/non-responders p=0.014.

analyses.code: ttest within subgroup by response status across feature_022, feature_024, feature_027, feature_029, feature_011
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup: feature_011 p=0.41, feature_022 p=0.35, feature_024 p=0.34, feature_027 p=0.84, feature_029 p=0.10. None significant; baseline characteristics within the responsive subgroup do not strongly stratify response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The full conjunctive rule generalizes: in a random 50/50 split of the cohort, the subgroup yields ~similar response rate in both halves (>=70%), versus ~16% in the complement of each half.

analyses.code: df.sample(frac=1, random_state=42); recompute subgroup means in half1 and half2
analyses.result_summary: Half1: n_subgroup=898, response 77.1%; complement 16.7%. Half2: n_subgroup=892, response 80.2%; complement 16.4%. Rule holds in both halves with similar magnitude.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A single formal multiplicative model captures the full pattern: y ~ const + a + b + c + s1 + s2 + ab + ac + bc + abc + abc*s1 + abc*s2, where (a,b,c)=(feature_001, feature_014, feature_021) and (s1,s2)=(feature_026, feature_009). The triple-product term abc has a large positive log-odds; abc*s1 and abc*s2 each cancel almost the entire abc effect.

Other binary features that do not enter the conjunctive rule (feature_010, feature_005, feature_016, feature_025, feature_019, feature_015, feature_018, feature_008, feature_006, feature_032, feature_030) do not interact strongly with the (a*b*c) compound either.

analyses.code: Logit interaction screen of subgroup indicator x each remaining feature
analyses.result_summary: Largest subgroup x feature interactions are with feature_024 (+0.13, p=0.03) and feature_004 (-0.17, p=0.05). All other remaining features show |coef|<0.4 and p>=0.06. No additional clean modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_024 within the responsive subgroup is associated with marginally higher response (positive interaction subgroup x feature_024), reversing the population-level direction; this is most likely a small artifact rather than a real reversal.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction subgroup*feature_024 = +0.126 (per SD, p=0.032). Within subgroup, response rate by feature_024 quartiles is 79%, 78%, 80%, 79% (nearly flat). Inside-population direction is essentially neutral; small p reflects large sample size. Not biologically compelling.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Heterogeneity searches restricted to single-flag or double-flag groupings (e.g., feature_001=1 alone with no suppressors) do not produce a comparable subgroup with elevated response.

analyses.code: Compare response in 1-of-3 and 2-of-3 flag combinations restricted to feature_026=0 & feature_009=0
analyses.result_summary: Restricted to no suppressors: 0/3 -> 17.2% (n=9626); 1/3 -> 16.2% (n=17087); 2/3 -> 16.2% (n=9818); 3/3 -> 78.6% (n=1790). Even with the suppressors absent, partial flag combinations remain at baseline. The treatment-like signal exists only when ALL THREE flags are co-present.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 78.6% response rate in the responsive subgroup is not driven by a hidden severity stratum: within the subgroup, mean feature_011 (age) is similar to the complement (~68 yrs), and severity covariates (feature_022, feature_024, feature_027) are similar (no positive selection of low-burden patients into the subgroup).

analyses.result_summary: Mean values inside vs outside subgroup: feature_011 67.8 vs 68.0; feature_022 20.4 vs 20.2; feature_024 60.4 vs 60.0; feature_027 5.99 vs 6.08; feature_004 0.81 vs 0.80; feature_020 3.91 vs 3.85. Nearly identical baseline -- the subgroup is not a low-severity stratum, supporting an interaction-driven (rather than confounded) interpretation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final subgroup statement: in patients with feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0, the rate of objective_response is ~78.6% (95% CI ~76.7-80.5%, n=1790), versus ~16.5% in all other patients. The treatment-like effect is strictly conjunctive in (feature_001, feature_014, feature_021) and is suppressed by the presence of either feature_026 or feature_009. No other feature modifies the within-subgroup response by more than ~10 pp.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup n=1790, responses=1407, response rate 0.786 (Wilson 95% CI 0.767-0.803). Complement n=48210, responses=7968, response rate 0.165. Adjusted OR (controlling feature_004, feature_020, feature_024, feature_022, feature_027, feature_029) = 20.1 (z=50.2). Subgroup x feature_004 interaction modestly reduces effect at higher feature_004 (interaction p=0.05), but response rate stays >=67% even at feature_004=2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other plausible heterogeneity hypotheses (effect modification by age feature_011, feature_010 binary stratifier, hemoglobin feature_028, or feature_018/feature_008) are negative: there is no sex/age subgroup of comparable magnitude, no laboratory threshold that further refines the rule, and no second hidden subgroup with elevated response outside the conjunctive rule.

analyses.code: for f in features: examine Logit(y ~ f + subgroup_complement_only) within the complement of the best subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within the complement (n=48210, response 16.5%): largest residual effects after the conjunctive rule are feature_004 (per-level OR ~0.65, p<1e-90), feature_020 (per-SD OR ~0.86), feature_024 (per-SD OR ~0.91). No residual interaction or subgroup raises any complement subset above ~25-30% response. The conjunctive rule captures essentially all heterogeneity in this dataset; remaining variation is dominated by feature_004 and a few continuous severity markers acting additively.
